meta.name: Shawarma Roasted Vegetables
meta.subtitle: Sheet-pan roasted veg with shawarma spice, lemon, and garlic for bowls & platters

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalogs
notes: Designed as a shawarma platter/bowl side: caramelized edges, warm spice, bright finish.
Works year-round; swap vegetables by season without breaking the flavor profile.

summary: A colorful, caramelized sheet-pan mix of vegetables tossed in shawarma spice, garlic, and olive oil, finished with lemon and herbs. Built for shawarma bowls and platters alongside rice, sauces, and pickles.

optional.label: Wire rack (on sheet pan)
optional.notes: Optional; promotes airflow for extra browning, especially if vegetables are wet.

notes: Nut-free unless you add tahini/seed toppings at serving.
If serving with sauces, allergen profile depends on sauce (e.g., sesame in tahini).

ingredients.label: Cauliflower florets
ingredients.notes: About 1 medium head, cut into bite-size florets.

ingredients.label: Zucchini, thick half-moons
ingredients.notes: Cut thicker than usual so it doesn’t get mushy.

ingredients.label: Carrots, diagonal slices
ingredients.notes: Cut similar thickness to ensure even roasting.

ingredients.label: Chickpeas, cooked (drained and dried)
ingredients.notes: Adds protein and crunch; pat dry for best browning.

ingredients.label: Shawarma spice blend
ingredients.notes: Use your SSA catalog blend (preferably salt-free).

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Adjust based on whether shawarma spice blend contains salt.

ingredients.label: Cilantro, chopped
ingredients.notes: Use parsley, cilantro, or a mix.

steps.title: Preheat oven and prep pan
instructions: Heat oven to 450°F (232°C).
Line a sheet pan with parchment (optional) for easier cleanup.
If your oven runs hot, use 425°F (218°C) and roast a few minutes longer.

steps.title: Cut vegetables for even roasting
instructions: Cut cauliflower into bite-size florets.
Slice zucchini into thick half-moons.
Slice peppers into strips; cut red onion into wedges.
Slice carrots on a diagonal (similar thickness to cauliflower stems).
Optional: drain chickpeas and pat dry very well.
checks: Vegetables cut to similar thickness for even roasting

steps.title: Toss with shawarma seasoning
instructions: In a large bowl, combine vegetables (and chickpeas if using).
Add olive oil, garlic, shawarma spice blend, optional turmeric, salt, pepper, and optional red pepper flakes.
Toss until everything is evenly coated.
checks: Even coating; no dry spice pockets

steps.title: Roast until browned and tender-crisp
instructions: Spread vegetables in a single layer on the sheet pan.
Roast 18–22 minutes, stirring/flipping once halfway through.
For extra char, broil 1–2 minutes at the end (watch closely).

steps.title: Finish with lemon and herbs
instructions: Remove pan from oven.
Immediately squeeze lemon juice over the hot vegetables.
Add lemon zest (optional) and toss gently.
Top with parsley/cilantro and a sprinkle of sumac (optional).
checks: Bright finish; seasoning balanced—add a pinch of salt if needed

steps.title: Serve as a platter or bowl component
instructions: Serve with shawarma meats, shawarma rice, pickles, and sauces like toum or tahini.
Great in bowls with cucumbers, tomatoes, chopped lettuce, and a drizzle of sauce.

fridge.notes: Cool fully and store airtight. Reheat on a hot sheet pan or skillet to re-crisp edges.

freezer.notes: Freeze flat for quick reheats. Texture softens slightly—best for bowls, wraps, or mixing into rice.

reheat.oven: 425°F (218°C) for 8–12 minutes on a sheet pan
reheat.skillet: Medium-high with a little oil, 5–7 minutes
reheat.microwave: Fast but softer; finish in a hot pan if you want crisp edges

batchNotes: Double batch on two sheet pans (rotate halfway).
Keep lemon/herbs separate for meal-prep; add after reheating for brighter flavor.
If serving with salty meats/sauces, start with 1 tsp salt and adjust after roasting.

makeAhead.best: Roast up to 3 days ahead; re-crisp in oven before serving.
makeAhead.dayOf: Add lemon + herbs right before serving.

notes: Vegetable swaps: eggplant, mushrooms, sweet potato, broccoli, green beans.
If using watery vegetables (mushrooms, zucchini), roast at 450°F and avoid crowding to prevent steaming.
Key to browning: hot oven + single layer + dry vegetables (especially chickpeas).
If your sheet pan is crowded, use two pans—crowding steams the vegetables.
Finish is everything: lemon + herbs make the shawarma spice pop.
Use carrots + sweet potato + red onion + cauliflower.
Roast 25–35 minutes, stirring once, until sweet potato is tender and browned.
Pat chickpeas very dry; add 1 additional tbsp olive oil and 1 tsp cornstarch (optional).
Roast chickpeas on a separate pan if you want maximum crisp.
Toss roasted vegetables with 1–2 tsp harissa paste thinned with a little lemon juice and olive oil.
Or drizzle with chili oil before serving.
